title: Linear Algebra
description: Covers matrix theory and linear algebra with emphasis on topics useful across disciplines — vector spaces, eigenvalues, linear transformations, and applications in science and engineering.

title: Introduction to Computer Science and Programming in Python
description: MIT's gateway CS course for students with no prior programming experience. Teaches computational thinking, algorithms, data structures, and problem solving using Python.

title: Introduction to Algorithms
instructor: Prof. Erik Demaine, Prof. Jason Ku
description: Rigorous introduction to algorithm design and analysis. Topics include sorting, searching, graph algorithms, dynamic programming, and computational complexity.

title: Multivariable Calculus
description: Covers differentiation and integration of functions of several variables, vectors and matrices, partial derivatives, multiple integrals, and vector calculus (Green's, Stokes', Divergence theorems).

title: Artificial Intelligence
description: Introduces the basic knowledge representation, problem solving, and learning methods of artificial intelligence. Covers search, constraint propagation, neural nets, and support vector machines.

title: Physics I: Classical Mechanics
description: Legendary lectures covering Newtonian mechanics, kinematics, dynamics, energy, momentum, and rotational motion. Famous for Professor Lewin's live demonstrations that bring physics to life.

title: Mathematics for Computer Science
instructor: Prof. Zachary Abel, Prof. Erik Demaine
description: Covers elementary discrete mathematics for CS: proofs, induction, graph theory, probability, and number theory. Provides the mathematical foundations essential for advanced CS study.

title: Probabilistic Systems Analysis and Applied Probability
description: An introduction to probability theory applied to engineering and science. Covers discrete and continuous random variables, Bayesian inference, Markov chains, and limit theorems.

title: Introduction to Electrical Engineering and Computer Science I
description: Integrated introduction to EECS using mobile robots. Combines programming, circuits, signals, and systems thinking in a hands-on, project-based approach unique to MIT's curriculum.

title: Statistics for Applications
description: Graduate-level statistics covering parameter estimation, hypothesis testing, linear regression, Bayesian statistics, and high-dimensional data analysis with real-world applications.
